title: Rankett Meetup Prep Guide — GTM Bible
html:
Rankett Meetup Prep Guide — GTM Bible

Source: 2026-01-07 Apple Note prepared for the GEO & AI Visibility Meetup, January 2026. ~25KB. The most comprehensive single articulation of Rankett's positioning Yahir has written.

TL;DR

  • What rankett is: a done-for-you white-label ai visibility engine for marketing/SEO agencies. NOT a SaaS tool. NOT for end clients. ONLY for agencies with 5+ existing clients.
  • The economics: $998/seat/month from agency to Rankett. Agency charges client $3K–$6K/mo. Spread = $2K–$5K of pure margin per seat.
  • The category: "ai search optimization" — visibility across chatgpt, Claude, gemini, perplexity, grok, deepseek, google ai overviews. "Prompts are the new keywords."
  • The core architecture: ghost white label — zero Rankett branding anywhere (UI, page source, meta tags, error messages). Agency's domain, logo, colors. Clients never know Rankett exists.
  • The moat: pooled data network effect — every new agency benefits from accumulated results across the entire Rankett network. New agencies can sell with proof from old agencies' data.
  • The filter: 5+ clients required + 60 day activation rule (must onboard 1 paying client in 60 days or lose access). Filters action-takers from tire-kickers.

The three-user model

User What they see What they do
Rankett (internal) Admin systems Fulfillment work, tracks implementations
Agency Admin portal Manages clients, runs analyses, customizes branding
End client White-labeled dashboard Views score + progress (read-only)

What's standardized vs custom

Standardized (always the same): platform setup, weekly reporting (first 4 weeks), monthly reporting after, auto-refresh analysis every 10 days, monthly PDF reports, schema markup for ai optimization, llm txt implementation, directory management across 50+ sites.

Never custom: writing agency's ads/emails/landing pages, end-client support, custom features per agency. "Product, not a dev shop."

Partner tiers

  • Tier 1 (1–4 clients): baseline email support
  • Tier 2 (5–9): faster responses, call availability
  • Tier 3 (10+): priority support, roadmap input

The old-vs-new evolution

Old New
Direct clients + agencies simultaneously One avatar: agencies w/ 5+ clients
Mixed offers, scattered messaging One offer: $998/seat white-label
Multiple entry points One funnel: landing → call → approve/reject
Ad-hoc fulfillment + scope creep Standardized fulfillment, no custom work
Each client a separate effort Compounding proof across all partners

Onboarding journey (the funnel)

  1. Apply (confirm 5+ clients)
  2. 15-min discovery call
  3. Approval + immediate white-label access
  4. Onboarding/implementation call
  5. Onboard first client
  6. Ongoing fulfillment

Open questions

  • How does this prep guide intersect with the actual Jan 2026 meetup outcome? (Worth ingesting any post-meetup notes.)
  • Has the 60 day activation rule been tested at scale yet — what's the actual conversion of approved → activated agencies?
  • The pooled data network effect is asserted but is the data actually shared across agencies in product, or only in the sales pitch?
  • What's the current agency count + per-agency client count? (Crosses with the /admin/fulfillment workstream from the 2026-04-25 audit.)

Related

  • aeo competitor arc — every defensive choice in this GTM bible (white-label-only, agency-only, $998/seat above Peekaboo's $200, 60-day activation rule) is a direct response to the Oct–Nov 2025 Peekaboo collision

Why this matters

This is Yahir's clearest articulation of rankett's thesis. Every future ingest about Rankett strategy/sales/positioning should backlink here. It's also the cleanest example of his GTM thinking pattern: pick a sharp avatar, standardize ruthlessly, monetize the leverage.
